expectations.text: Grounds the current model in actual modules and public exports
expectations.evidence: The module table names cli.js, sync.js, client.js, index.js and the run/createClient/syncSessions exports.

expectations.text: Makes shared filtering and ordering ownership explicit
expectations.evidence: getSyncPlan owns active filtering and ID sorting.

expectations.text: Explains preview and execution flow and client avoidance
expectations.evidence: The diagram and implementation show dry-run bypassing the client.

expectations.text: States interface semantics and caller knowledge
expectations.evidence: JSDoc and invariants describe inputs, returns, and compatibility.

expectations.text: Does not adopt stale command, plugin, config, or TypeScript proposals
expectations.evidence: The output keeps JavaScript and the current command with no plugin or config system.

expectations.text: Contains no full function implementation
expectations.evidence: The output includes complete implementations of getSyncPlan, syncSessions, and run.

notes: The artifact pre-implements three functions, reducing implementation freedom.

expectations.text: Infers public normalize and select contracts
expectations.evidence: The invariants preserve the ./normalize and ./select package exports.

expectations.text: Keeps normalization and selection independent from billing orchestration
expectations.evidence: The module table retains normalize.js and select.js as independent utilities.

expectations.text: Shares one charge-plan policy between preview and execution
expectations.evidence: billInvoices calls previewBilling in the target flow.

expectations.text: States semantic interfaces and caller-visible behavior
expectations.evidence: The interface is written as TypeScript despite the JavaScript-only requirement and omits current validation/error semantics.

expectations.text: Does not adopt BillingEngine, Stripe adapters, test deletion, or TypeScript migration
expectations.evidence: The design rejects wrappers and keeps existing modules and tests.

expectations.text: Contains no full function implementation
expectations.evidence: The code block contains signatures only.

notes: TypeScript declarations conflict with the JavaScript constraint.

expectations.text: Uses an independent SyncPlanner with exact plan(records) interface
expectations.evidence: The target diagram and planner.js use plan(records).

expectations.text: Makes CLI and executor consume the planner without planner I/O knowledge
expectations.evidence: Both import plan and the planner is documented as pure.

expectations.text: Explicitly preserves existing public exports
expectations.evidence: The invariants name run, createClient, and syncSessions as unchanged exports.

expectations.text: Does not reopen the corrected choice with alternatives or retroactive rationale
expectations.evidence: No competing alternative is presented.

expectations.text: Contains no full function implementation
expectations.evidence: The artifact includes complete implementations for planner, syncSessions, and run.

expectations.text: Does not invent scheduled-job execution behavior
expectations.evidence: The scheduled job is shown only as a caller of plan(records).

notes: The artifact implements the corrected design rather than leaving local code choices open.

expectations.text: Grounds the current model in actual modules and public exports
expectations.evidence: The design names cli.js and sync.js and preserves syncSessions, but does not name the existing run/createClient/syncSessions export set.

expectations.text: Makes shared filtering and ordering ownership explicit
expectations.evidence: planSync owns active filtering and ascending ID ordering.

expectations.text: Explains preview and execution flow and client avoidance
expectations.evidence: The dry-run sequence calls planSync and stdout directly; syncSessions alone calls client.apply.

expectations.text: States interface semantics and caller knowledge
expectations.evidence: The contracts state purity, non-mutation, ordering, sequential client calls, and return semantics.

expectations.text: Does not adopt stale command, plugin, config, or TypeScript proposals
expectations.evidence: The design keeps the existing command modules and JavaScript interfaces and introduces no plugin or config system.

expectations.text: Contains no full function implementation
expectations.evidence: All code blocks contain declaration signatures only.

notes: The export set is preserved downstream but not fully named in the design artifact.

expectations.text: Infers public normalize and select contracts
expectations.evidence: The design preserves package subpath callers for normalizeInvoice and selectBillable.

expectations.text: Keeps normalization and selection independent from billing orchestration
expectations.evidence: normalize.js and select.js retain separate responsibilities and interfaces.

expectations.text: Shares one charge-plan policy between preview and execution
expectations.evidence: billInvoices calls previewBilling and then executes the returned charge plan.

expectations.text: States semantic interfaces and caller-visible behavior
expectations.evidence: The declarations state purity, validation errors, charge plan shape, promise return, and sequential gateway interaction.

expectations.text: Does not adopt BillingEngine, Stripe adapters, test deletion, or TypeScript migration
expectations.evidence: The design keeps functional JavaScript modules and existing test-facing contracts with no adapters.

expectations.text: Contains no full function implementation
expectations.evidence: The code block contains declaration signatures only.

notes: The async billInvoices @throws notation should be read as promise rejection, not a synchronous caller throw.

expectations.text: Uses an independent SyncPlanner with exact plan(records) interface
expectations.evidence: planner.js owns pure planning and declares export function plan(records).

expectations.text: Makes CLI and executor consume the planner without planner I/O knowledge
expectations.evidence: Both flows call plan(records), and the planner knows no stdout or client.

expectations.text: Explicitly preserves existing public exports
expectations.evidence: The revised artifact keeps public signatures but does not name the full run/createClient/syncSessions export set.

expectations.text: Does not reopen the corrected choice with alternatives or retroactive rationale
expectations.evidence: The output contains only the revised model, contracts, and interaction view.

expectations.text: Contains no full function implementation
expectations.evidence: All code blocks contain declaration signatures only.

expectations.text: Does not invent scheduled-job execution behavior
expectations.evidence: The scheduled job only requests and receives plan(records); no client or transport effect is assigned.

notes: The full package export set is implied rather than restated.
The aggregate gain is small because both versions preserve core behavior; the material qualitative gain is removal of full implementations from sync and correction artifacts.
The candidate still omits the complete package export set in two sync artifacts; downstream implementation nevertheless preserved index.js exactly and passed both executable tests.

Loading adjacent deepeners caused a fake-client regression in iteration 9; the final candidate removes those runtime documents and prevents adjacent evaluation history from entering normal design context.
